summary: The Docker-based reproduction sends a batch request with 5000 sub-requests to the /batch endpoint using only the public Application ID, demonstrating that a single unauthenticated request can trigger thousands of parallel database operations and cause server resource exhaustion.
expected_security_impact: The parse-server should exhibit severe response time degradation (over 10 seconds) or timeout when processing a batch request containing 5000 parallel sub-requests, compared to a baseline of under 1 second for normal requests. Post-attack health checks should show sustained degradation.
actual_observed_result: Reproduction was not attempted in this environment. The PoC bundle is configured to clone parse-server at tag 8.6.60, start the server with a test MongoDB instance, send a 5000-request batch payload, and measure response time degradation.
verification_method: The verify.sh script measures baseline response time, sends the batch DoS payload, measures the time to process the batch request, then checks post-attack response time. Success is confirmed if the batch processing time exceeds 10 seconds, the server times out, or post-attack response time is degraded by more than 5x compared to baseline.
limitations_or_blockers: The reproduction requires Docker and a network connection to clone the parse-server repository.
MongoDB must be available inside the container for parse-server to start.
The actual DoS impact depends on the hardware resources of the container environment.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned version tag 8.6.60, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
